Continues decline; ring's end changes nothing for him

seasonal_track.season: Late August / early September — hot, humid. Lakeview's AC unreliable.
September / October — cooling, leaves turning on courtyard trees.
Late October / November — cold, shorter days, parking lot dark by 5 PM.

key_events.event: Ring begins hoarding meds after pharmacy switch

geography.primary_setting: Lakeview Gardens Rehabilitation and Nursing Center, suburban central New Jersey

key_locations.name: Wing A (long-term care)
key_locations.details: Single-story, L-shaped. Rooms 100-120A. Harry 114A (single), Agnes 108A, Lola 112A, Earl/Burt 116A. Long hallway, sub-station can't see far end.

key_locations.name: TV room
key_locations.details: Farthest common area from nurse's station. Vinyl chairs, wall TV, card table. Ring meets here after midnight. Not visible from overnight aide's desk.

key_locations.name: Nurse's station / medication room
key_locations.details: Central, between wings. Controlled substance log, locked cabinet, count sheets. Sub-station near Wing A entrance.

key_locations.name: Courtyard
key_locations.details: Paved, three deciduous trees, benches, parking lot view. No garden. Tammy's break spot; Lola-Tammy scene location.

key_locations.name: Loading dock
key_locations.details: Back of building. Not visible from entrance. Where Agnes's notebooks are burned (chapter 17).

key_locations.name: Strip mall parking lot
key_locations.details: Marcus's distribution point. ~20 min from Lakeview.

key_locations.name: Cemetery
key_locations.details: Maryann Washington's grave. ~40 min by facility van. Drop point for Earl-Marcus exchanges.

distances: Carmen's apartment to Gloria's house: ~15 min
Lakeview to cemetery: ~40 min by van
Sub-station to TV room: full wing length, not visible
Parking lot to TV room windows: visible if lights on (Tammy sees this, chapter 14)

world_rules: Controlled substances require per-shift count log, signed by departing and arriving nurse. Discrepancies documented under dispensing nurse's name.
LPNs dispense under RN/physician orders. All doses logged under Carmen's license number.
Medication diversion is federal crime (Controlled Substances Act) and grounds for license revocation.
State DOH surveys are annual, timing unannounced. Surveyors interview staff/residents, review med records.
Residents may refuse medications (Agnes's evening refusal is legal).
Fentanyl patches have lethal overdose risk for opioid-naive users. Agnes's bright ethical line.
Visiting hours 10 AM-8 PM, loosely enforced. Visitor log rarely cross-referenced.
Overnight staffing: one aide (Devon) and one nurse for both wings. Devon typically sleeps.
Facility van available for monthly outings for capable residents.

power_structure.mechanism: sole person who can leave building
holds information and standing to report

objects.name: Hungarian-English dictionary
objects.origin: Gift from wife Erzsébet. Hardcover, hollowed out.
objects.status: Pill stash in 116A. Carmen moves it to supply closet chapter 11.

objects.name: Cookie tin
objects.status: Handoff container for Marcus. Destroyed chapter 17.

objects.name: Shared prepaid phone
objects.origin: Purchased by Marcus, kept in Harry's room.
objects.status: Broken and dumped chapter 17. Agnes intercepts misdirected text chapter 16.

objects.name: Agnes's notebooks
objects.origin: Created post-recruitment. Inventory, buyer needs, interactions, expirations.

objects.name: Carmen's license badge
objects.status: Active throughout. She adjusts it under stress.

objects.name: Lola's gold crucifix
objects.status: Worn throughout. Touches it under stress.

objects.name: Earl's handkerchief
objects.status: Checks for blood after coughing. No blood through chapter 15.

debts.status: Harry paying down from ring earnings

income_sources.reliability: Stable if license maintained. Doubles add $500-800/month.

knowledge_state.knows: Ring's existence, membership, operation, buyers, finances; she lied to state surveyor
Carmen discovered the ring; the full origin story (pharmacy switch, dangerous drug interaction)
She is cognitively intact and wrongly institutionalized
Something is wrong — Carmen's behavior, count issues, residents' inconsistent confusion, TV room light at 12:30 AM
His cough is serious and worsening

hidden_from: the money comes from drug sales

knowledge_state.knows: Took volume buyer deal; has 'another way' (chapter 18 voicemail)
Harry makes private phone call after cleanup
Her mother had a stroke and needs care
hidden_from: Agnes has recovered and is fully competent

environmental_constants: Lakeview smells: disinfectant over urine baseline; institutional food at mealtimes; talcum powder; hand sanitizer every fifteen feet; warm-skin smell of people who don't go outside.

Lakeview sounds: call bells, TV room programming (game shows morning, soaps afternoon, news evening), ventilation hum, soft moaning from Room 110, wheels on linoleum. Night: much quieter, sounds carry, building creaks.
Lakeview lighting: fluorescent, some flickering. TV room warmer from screen glow. Night hallways at half-light.
Courtyard: three deciduous trees (lose leaves Oct/Nov), paved, four benches, parking lot view. No garden.
Medication room: off central station. Locked cabinet, count log on clipboard. Smells of alcohol wipes and pill dust.
